title: “Homework 6” output: html_document ———————

Part 2a: House Price Trends

ggplot(house_prices, aes(x = year, y = house_price_index)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~state) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020),
                     labels = c("80", "00", "20"))

Part 2b: Reshape Data

house_reshaped <- house_prices %>%
  gather(key = "measure",
         value = "value",
         -date, -state, -year)

head(house_reshaped)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   date       state    year measure       value
##   <date>     <chr>   <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>
## 1 1976-01-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.7
## 2 1976-02-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.7
## 3 1976-03-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.6
## 4 1976-04-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.5
## 5 1976-05-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.4
## 6 1976-06-01 Alabama  1976 unemploy_perc   6.5

Part 2c: Combined Plot

ggplot(house_reshaped, aes(x = year, y = value, color = measure)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~state)

Comment

This graph is not ideal because house price index and unemployment percentage are on very different scales. Plotting them together makes it difficult to interpret trends clearly.
